Wasl Group launches a unique artistic initiative titled “Museum of Light” to showcase creative and artistic works across the group’s assets. The first collaboration under the initiative was implemented with Emirati artist Mattar bin Lahej, featuring an artwork displayed on the façade of Wasl Tower inspired by a poem by His Highness Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum. The initiative reflects the group’s commitment to supporting Dubai’s cultural and creative landscape and enhancing the presence of art within urban spaces. @waslgroup

Maktoum bin Mohammed: Today, I met with Louis Ferla, CEO of Cartier, and we explored opportunities for collaboration in the UAE’s luxury goods sector and ways to enhance Dubai’s position as a global destination for luxury retail. The UAE’s business environment supports global brand expansion, and a partnership-driven approach with the private sector is a key driver of our economic growth. Innovation and design are essential to enhancing the competitiveness of Dubai’s luxury retail sector and developing shopping experiences that meet the aspirations of its residents and visitors from around the world.

Iran Offers Services to Ships in #Strait_of_Hormuz 🔻Iran’s Ports and Maritime Organization (PMO) issued a formal notice to all shipmasters in the Strait of Hormuz and nearby waters. 🔻It offers food, fuel, medical services and limited permitted repair items. 🔻Ships can request assistance via VHF Channel 16, contacting the nearest Iranian port’s traffic control. 🔻The message will be repeated three times daily for three days.

Within the National Guard, we move forward with steadfast determination toward a future of greater readiness and professionalism.
